Confidential · Creative Labs · 2026
01 Two operating modes The eSplit concept
1
Replay · Replay
Performance
Analysis
Two recorded runs compared in synchronized split-screen. Reveals style differences, gap measurement, and positional advantage at every point of the course.
Input A — Replay server (Athlete 1)
Input B — Replay server (Athlete 2)
2
Live · Replay
Live
Comparison
One athlete runs live. The other ran before. eSplit synchronizes the live feed against the replay in real time — the broadcast never leaves the action.
Input A — Live camera feed
Input B — Replay server (reference)
3
New Medias · Viewer Choice · Sync & Zoom
Viewer
Choice
The broadcaster distributes video + sync data. Each viewer assembles their own split-screen — choosing reference athlete, sync mode, and zoom level.
Input A — Live camera feed
Input B — Replay server (reference)
02 Core Technology Sync Modes Real-time speed ramping · Frame-accurate output
Mode 0% → Action Sync
Real-time reframing & tracking: the engine continuously reframes and tracks each athlete independently — correcting camera differences so both channels share an identical crop, at all times.
Same action,
same instant

Both athletes are aligned on the same movement — same gate, same obstacle, same body position. The engine continuously calculates and applies individual speed ramps so that corresponding actions remain perfectly superimposed throughout the entire run.

Mode 100% → Chrono Sync
Real-time reframing & tracking: same AI reframing applies — both athletes are continuously reframed to the same crop, regardless of the original camera angles, throughout the entire run.
Same elapsed time,
different position

Both athletes are shown at the same clock time from start. One may be ahead of the other. The engine locks playback to elapsed time, making the time gap visually readable at any moment of the run.

The operator can blend between both modes at any time
0% ←————————→ 100%
03 Frame Track AI reframing — no operator
AI detects and reframes each athlete independently on both channels
04 Sync Live Sync Action-based — frame accurate
Continuous time-ramping — one input can be live
05 Edit Edit Slow motion · Action sync · Chrono sync
Speed, sync mode and reframing — switchable on demand — no changes to your broadcast chain
06 Second Screen Family 3 — User-driven
The viewer becomes
the director
The broadcaster distributes the video stream alongside synchronization metadata — no additional infrastructure required.
On any device, the viewer selects playback speed, sync mode, and reference athlete — including their own previous run.
Technical data layers — speed, angles, splits — can be aggregated by your second-screen production teams.
The output stream carries an embedded authentication layer, limiting unauthorized redistribution.
07 Architecture Network Broadcast · eSplit Server · Second Screen
WEC Supplement WEC Lab Results First evaluation — single sector
VIMEO VIDEO
TO BE INSERTED
Laboratory · WEC Evaluation
First Results
Single Sector

We have conducted a first laboratory evaluation using WEC race footage, focusing on a single sector of the circuit.

This example is intended to demonstrate the performance level we can achieve today, rather than the ideal scenario or final production framing.

We apologise for the image quality — this footage was not processed from broadcast-grade material. Please view this as a basis for discussion.

WEC Sequence 1 Trajectory Sync Same position · Same line
Action Sync · Position Alignment
Same Position,
Same Trajectory

This sequence aligns both cars at the same position on the track, demonstrating that they are following an identical trajectory through the sector.

The action synchronisation mode locks both feeds to the same moment of movement, making the driving line directly comparable.

WEC Sequence 2 Sector Advantage Time delta · Two apex points
Chrono Sync · Delta Analysis
Where the Faster
Car Gains Time

This sequence reveals at which point in the S-section the faster car builds its advantage — visible at two distinct apex points.

The time delta is derived directly from the synchronisation engine — no external timing data required.

WEC Sequence 3 Sting View Distinctive editorial format
Sting Format · Editorial
A Distinctive
Visual Format

Our system also enables sting-style sequences — a specific editorial format that creates a more impactful visual presentation of the comparison.

WEC Sequence 3b Extended Sting Explicit · Discussion basis
Extended Format · WEC Application
A More Explicit
Presentation

Based on the same sequence, this extended sting format produces a more explicit comparison — potentially better suited to on-screen storytelling.

We remain fully open to your editorial preferences and welcome your suggestions on the scenarios you would like to explore.

We remain available for any question
or test scenario
